title: lepidium virginicum var. menziesii
intro: Lepidium virginicum var. menziesii, also known as Western Peppergrass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae), native to the western regions of North America. Western Peppergrass has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, ease digestive issues, and support immune function. Western Peppergrass can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in salves and poultices to treat skin conditions and wounds.
